matches.name: J. C. Penney
matches.description: Department-store peer in the same secular-decline regime. Lagging position, no durable moat, repeated management turnover.

matches.name: Best Buy
matches.description: Specialty retail facing e-commerce disruption — but with execution turnaround under Hubert Joly. Counter-example to the Sears thesis.

matches.name: RadioShack
matches.description: Specialty electronics retail, eroding scale, real-estate-heavy footprint, financial engineering rather than operational reinvention.

matches.name: Kmart
matches.description: Discount retail, scale erosion vs. Walmart/Target, no merchandising differentiation. Pre-Sears-merger Kmart trajectory.

matches.name: Borders Group
matches.description: Bricks-and-mortar book retail, Amazon disruption, late digital pivot. Pattern: dominant category share in a disrupted category.

matches.name: Macy's
matches.description: Department-store peer. Stronger merchandising and digital pivot than Sears; survived but no durable outperformance.

pullquote_html: Five of the six closest analogues to SHLD-2014 ended in bankruptcy or substantial value destruction. The one positive outcome (Best Buy) required an explicit operational reinvention — exactly what was absent in Sears's leadership_quality grade.
